Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

SpTU 2, 025 [Ušburruda][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
o 1o 1

EN₂ man-nu ib-tuq ID₂ u₂-šar-da a-⸢tap-pi⸣

(o 1) Incantation: "Who diverted the river, caused my small canal to flow out? (Who) let the waters of my river meadows dry up?

o 22

saḫ-ḫa-a-ti ša₂ ID₂-ia u₂-ša₂-bi-la A-MEŠ

o 33

bur-x-a-nu ABZU bi-nu-ut A.AB.⸢BA⸣

(o 3) ... of the Apsu, creation of the sea, diverted the river, caused my small canal to flow out, let the waters of my river meadows dry up.

o 44

ib-tuq ID₂ u₂-šar-da a-tap-⸢pi⸣

o 55

saḫ-ḫa-a-ti ša₂ ID₂-ia u₂-ša₂-bi-la A-⸢MEŠ⸣

o 66

man-nu lu-uš-pur ana EN re-me-ni-i dAMAR.UTU

(o 6) Whom should I send to the merciful lord, Marduk?

o 77

li-še-bi-la gišŠINIG bi-nu-ut d⸢ti⸣-[amat]

(o 7) May he send me the tamarisk, creation of Tiamat!

o 88

KI.MIN<(li-še-bi-la)> u₂IN.NU.UŠ lil-li-di!(KUR) d⸢ti⸣-[amat]

(o 8) Ditto (may he send me) the maštakal-plant, offspring of Tiamat!

o 99

KI.MIN<(li-še-bi-la)> šimLI se-riq d⸢ti⸣-amat

(o 9) Ditto (may he send me) the burāšu-juniper, offering for Tiamat!

o 1010

KI.MIN<(li-še-bi-la)> giššim!(ŠE.)GAM.ME U₂ KU!? lil-li-[di ...]1

(o 10) Ditto (may he send me) the ṣumlalû-plant, the ... plant, offspring of [...]!

o 1111

KI.MIN<(li-še-bi-la)> PIŠ₁₀.dID₂ la ma-ḫi-⸢rat⸣ [...]

(o 11) Ditto (may he send me) the unrivalled sulphur [...]!

o 1212

KI.MIN<(li-še-bi-la)> šimGUR₂.GUR₂ ša₂ KUR x x [...]

(o 12) Ditto (may he send me) the kurkuru-tree of the land ... [...]!

o 1313

KI.MIN<(li-še-bi-la)> SI DARA₃.⸢MAŠ⸣ [...]

(o 13) Ditto (may he send me) the stag's-horn (plant) [...]!

o 1414

lum-ḫaṣ ina šil-ta-ḫi x [...]

(o 14) Let me hit ... with an arrow [...]!

Reverse
r 1r 1

li-is-se-⸢ker ID₂⸣ li-ma-[...]

(r 1) May the river be dammed! ... [...]

r 22

saḫ-ḫa-a-ti ša₂ ID₂-ia la uš-⸢tab?⸣-[ba-la]

(r 2) May my river meadows not be dried up!

r 33

li-rid LU₂ TUR.TUR ina IRI lil-sa-a [...]

(r 3) May a very young man descend! May he shout [...] in the city!

r 44

KA.INIM.MA UŠ₁₁.BUR₂.RU.DA u NAM.⸢ERIM₂⸣.[BUR₂.RU.DA]

(r 4) Wording of the ušburrudû-ritual (to dispel sorcery) and the namerimburrudû-ritual (to remove a curse).

r 55

DU₃.DU₃-BI gišŠINIG u₂IN.NU.UŠ šimLI ⸢giš⸣[šimGAM.ME]

(r 5) Its ritual: tamarisk (wood), maštakal-plant, burāšu-juniper (wood), ṣumlalû-plant, sulphur, kukuru-wood, stag's-horn (plant), 7 plants [...]: you dry (them) up; you crush (them); you sieve (them). In the morning, in front of Šamaš, you recite the incantation 7 times on top of (them).

r 66

PIŠ₁₀.dID₂ šimGUR₂.GUR₂ SI DARA₃.MAŠ 7 U₂-[ḪI.A ...]

r 77

ḪAD₂.DU GAZ SIM ina še-ri₃ ina IGI dUTU EN₂ 7šu₂ ana UGU ⸢ŠID⸣

r 88

ina šil-ta-ḫu ZABAR 7šu₂ SIG₃as-su₂ lu-u₂ ina KAŠ SAG

(r 8) You hit him (the patient) 7 times with a bronze arrow; again and again, you make (him) drink (it) either with first-class beer or with water or with wine.

r 99

lu-u₂ ina A-MEŠ lu-u₂ ina gišGEŠTIN NAG-MEŠ-ma

r 1010

NAM.ERIM₂ BUR₂ir kiš-pi ana ⸢LU₂⸣ NU TE

(r 10) May the curse be dispelled! Witchcraft will not approach the man!

(catchline)
r 1111

EN₂ dGIŠ-gim₂-maš LUGAL KIti₃ DAGALti₃ DI.KU₅ la ṭa-ti₃

(r 11) Incantation: "Gilgameš, king of the extensive Underworld, incorruptible judge!"

(colophon)
r 1212

IM BAša₂-a bu₁₂-kur₂ mdINANA—MU—KAM ŠA₃.BAL.BAL mE₂.KUR—za-kir lu₂MAŠ.MAŠ

(r 12) Tablet of Iqiša, son of Ištar-šum-ereš, descendant of Ekur-Zakir.

1A sign has been erased between U₂ and KU.
